items.text: ConventionsConfig struct defined in a conventions module under crates/norn/src/tools/
ConventionsConfig parsed from TOML using toml crate with #[derive(Deserialize)]

ConventionGroup struct has fields: tools (Vec<String>), paths (Vec<String>), loc (Option<LocCheck>), advise_on (Vec<String>), block_on (Vec<String>), bypass_detection (Option<bool>), adapters_dir (Option<PathBuf>), priority (i32 default 0)
LocCheck struct has fields: limit (u64), handling (Handling enum: Advise or Block)
ConventionsConfig::load(path) reads and parses CONVENTIONS.toml, returning Result<ConventionsConfig, ConventionsError>
ConventionsConfig::load returns a typed error (ConventionsError) for missing file, parse failure, and invalid glob patterns
DiagnosticInfra gains conventions: Option<ConventionsConfig> field
ConventionsConfig is parsed at session startup and stored on DiagnosticInfra before any tool execution
Convention groups are matched by tool name (current tool in tools list) AND file path (modified file matches any paths glob)
Glob patterns in paths are compiled at load time using the globset crate, same as AdapterRegistry
Multiple convention groups can match the same file; all matching groups run their checks
When multiple groups specify conflicting LOC limits, the group with the highest priority field wins
Groups at the same priority with conflicting LOC limits: first-match wins, warning logged at startup
Path matching is relative to the workspace root stored on DiagnosticInfra
If no convention group matches a file, no post-mutation quality checks run for that file
PostCheckResult struct defined with fields: outcome (PostValidateOutcome), advisories (Vec<Advisory>)
Advisory struct defined with fields: message (String), source (String)
RuntimePostValidateCheck::check() return type changed from PostValidateOutcome to PostCheckResult
ToolRegistry::execute_tool post-check loop updated to handle PostCheckResult: outcome processed as before, advisories collected separately
Advisories injected into tool output under an advisories key by the registry
Advisories do not set is_error on the tool output
Advisories do not cause PostValidateOutcome::Fail
Adapter results from advise_on list produce Advisory items (informational)
Adapter results from block_on list produce errors that trigger PostValidateOutcome::Fail
LOC check with handling = advise produces Advisory items
LOC check with handling = block produces errors that trigger PostValidateOutcome::Fail
Tool's own AST validation (tree-sitter parse check) remains unconditional and always blocks, unaffected by conventions
When conventions is Some, DiagnosticsPostCheck uses convention groups exclusively for post-mutation checks
When conventions is None, DiagnosticsPostCheck runs no post-mutation quality checks (only structural AST validation from tool itself)
Hardcoded 500 LOC limit removed from diagnostics_check.rs
Hardcoded 200 LOC limit for mod.rs/lib.rs/main.rs removed from diagnostics_check.rs
Hardcoded clippy check removed from diagnostics_check.rs
Hardcoded bypass detection removed from diagnostics_check.rs
DiagnosticsPostCheck iterates matching convention groups and dispatches adapter checks per advise_on/block_on lists
bypass_detection defaults to true for Rust and TypeScript, false for other languages
Adapter names in advise_on/block_on resolve against the AdapterRegistry on DiagnosticInfra
Unresolvable adapter names log a warning at startup but do not prevent other conventions from loading
Declarative TOML adapters from adapters_dir are loaded and registered on AdapterRegistry alongside compiled adapters
Custom adapters from adapters_dir are sandboxed to subprocess execution only — no arbitrary code loading into the norn process
norn init conventions command exists as a CLI subcommand
Init scans the project file tree, collects file extensions and their counts
Init checks which compiled adapters are available for each detected file type
Init checks which external tools are installed via which/where lookup (mypy, ruff, eslint, shellcheck, etc.)
Init generates convention groups with language-ecosystem-appropriate LOC limits
Init populates advise_on with available adapters, block_on empty by default
Init writes CONVENTIONS.toml with comments explaining each field
Init never overwrites an existing CONVENTIONS.toml — appends missing groups or writes to a new path
ToolError::PostValidationFailed carries optional committed_output so blocking convention checks preserve the tool's structured output (PR0)
PostCheckResult type exists so advisory convention results flow through output without triggering Fail (PR1)
CONVENTIONS.toml is optional — projects without one get no post-mutation quality checks
LOC counting uses tokei (language-aware, not raw wc -l)
Glob matching uses the globset crate, same as search tool and AdapterRegistry
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
